steps.title: Identify the tense the sentence requires
steps.content: The sentence describes what Lincoln did after winning the 1860 presidential election — a completed historical action. Historians are noted as praising it, meaning the action is entirely in the past. The blank needs a simple past tense verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘has leveraged’: Present perfect implies the action began in the past and continues into the present. Lincoln’s 1860 cabinet appointments are long completed, so this tense is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘is leveraging’: Simple present implies an ongoing or habitual action, but Lincoln’s appointments occurred in 1860 and cannot still be happening. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘will leverage’: Future tense implies the action has not yet happened, which contradicts the historical past-event context established in the first sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘leveraged’: Simple past correctly places the action at a completed point in the past (1860), matching the historical context throughout the passage. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
